entries.mem0ai/mem0: The official Hermes Agent memory provider offering a universal memory layer for user, session, and agent state. It supports hybrid retrieval via semantic, keyword, and entity matching across managed cloud or self-hosted deployments.
entries.supermemoryai/supermemory: An official Hermes Agent memory provider featuring a custom vector graph engine and byte-level deduplication for high-efficiency recall. It automates fact extraction and integrates with external connectors like Google Drive, Notion, and GitHub.
entries.volcengine/OpenViking: An official Hermes Agent memory provider that uses a tiered L0/L1/L2 loading system to significantly reduce token consumption. It organizes memories via a filesystem-based structure and provides visualized retrieval trajectories for debugging.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agent that synthesizes cited answers and performs explicit gap analysis on missing information. It utilizes a self-wiring knowledge graph to handle complex entity relationship queries and scoped multi-user access.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-focused memory system that implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent growth. It acts as an LLM wrapper that automates storage and retrieval across providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A reasoning-first memory library that extracts conclusions from conversations to build stateful agents. It employs peer-centric modeling and a hybrid search combining BM25 and vector retrieval.
entries.campfirein/byterover-cli: An official Hermes Agent memory provider that treats context as a git repository, enabling versioning via commits, branches, and merges. It features a 5-tier retrieval system for sub-100ms latency and supports cloud sync for team knowledge sharing.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive self-improving context harness that uses real-world evaluation and feedback loops to optimize agent performance. It generates persistent playbooks and structured execution traces across various providers including Claude Code and MCP.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system providing sub-millisecond retrieval latency using SQLite storage. It features a temporal knowledge graph and episodic memory compression with native MCP support for AI clients.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin that uses a hierarchical DAG to compact conversation history without losing detail. It provides specialized tools for grepping and expanding summaries stored in a local SQLite database.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for the Honcho library that enables private cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM and includes automated Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis setup scripts.
entries.Signet-AI/signetai: A local-first identity and secrets layer that provides portable state across Hermes Agent and other MCP clients. It features a repairable memory system allowing users to edit, delete, or reclassify agent context via SQLite.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer for multi-agent systems using an open YAML engram format to eliminate API costs. It utilizes ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts while decaying stale information over time.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: An on-device context engine that operates entirely locally without cloud dependencies or API keys. It combines BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs via MCP server and Claude Code hooks.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that increases prefill speed by 1.5–3× through prefix cache reuse. It reduces prompt token usage by approximately 36% and provides native plugin support for Hermes Agent.
entries.yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin: An embedded, self-maintaining memory plugin that provides explainable recall via 'why_retrieved' tags. It includes first-class contradiction tracking and canonicalization without requiring a separate server or GPU.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: A memory kernel stack that consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval. It leverages a multi-layered storage approach using SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma to provide bounded evidence packing.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system combining semantic search with ranked graph traversal. It automatically indexes local directories, URLs, and multimedia files using edge tags to create navigable relationships.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It uses local-first storage with SQLite-vec and GGUF models via an MCP-native server.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: A utility that reduces prompt token overhead by using local BM25 ranking to select only relevant tool schemas. It includes a dashboard to track token savings and is compatible with Hermes Agent v0.14.0.
entries.Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin: A local-only plugin using an embedded .lbdb graph database with importance-weighted recall to surface high-priority memories. It supports BM25 keyword search and optional GLiNER2 entity extraction.
entries.MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory: A pull-model episodic memory plugin that ensures complete deletion of events to prevent persistent summaries from haunting future recall. It provides a trace.jsonl audit log for every memory operation.
entries.futurebrowser/hermes-exabase-plugin: A plugin that integrates Exabase M-1 for self-organizing long-term agent memory. It provides tools for manual storage and searching, featuring configurable query expansion and result reranking.

entries.nexu-io/open-design: A local-first design and prototyping system that enables Hermes Agent to generate prototypes, motion graphics, and decks. It integrates with over 150 design systems and 22 local CLIs to bridge the gap between coding agents and visual design.
entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: A comprehensive library of 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major industry frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It uses the agentskills.io standard to bring professional security domains to agentic workflows.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill converts natural language and codebases into .drawio XML diagrams. It supports a wide array of presets including UML, ERD, and Architecture, utilizing over 10,000 official shapes.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: An agentic evolver that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It unifies skill libraries across different agents and teams while natively integrating with Hermes and OpenClaw.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: A content auditing tool that eliminates detectable AI writing patterns through rewrite, detect, and edit modes. It utilizes a tiered replacement table of 109 words and 10 phrases to ensure human-like output.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library of modular skills based on industry guidelines and established texts. It is compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io agents, offering installation via a plugin marketplace or npx.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose reusable skills. It generates both the necessary Python plugin scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation for seamless saving.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: Provides type-safe agentskills.io support for Pydantic AI. It employs progressive disclosure of skill metadata to reduce token usage and supports both programmatic and filesystem-based definitions.
entries.ZeroPointRepo/youtube-skills: A suite of tools for extracting YouTube transcripts, captions, and timestamps without relying on yt-dlp. It enables Hermes Agent to perform video searches, browse channels, and extract playlists.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and one-click installation of skills, featuring automated security scanning with 65+ threat detection rules. It includes a creator marketplace with crypto and Stripe payment support.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: A set of skills that teaches Hermes Agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions. It helps agents identify structural conservation laws and generate reports on analysis blind spots.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: A harness for long-running agents that provides structured memory and a plan-approve-execute lifecycle. It stores work and proof in local plain files to facilitate cross-feature learning.
entries.AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow: A pre-build workflow that transforms rough ideas into structured design documents and implementation handoffs. It offers Lite and Full modes and includes a spec review gate with PASS/FAIL evaluation.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: A literate programming skill that weaves code and documentation into .lit.md files. It uses a tangler to extract runnable source files and automatically syncs edits back to the literate document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: A memory management system that enables cross-instance shared memory via markdown files. It captures high-value decisions to create a pipeline for fine-tuning and switching to replacement models.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Provides agentskills.io compliant tools for interacting with the Chainlink oracle network. It supports integrations for CCIP, VRF, Data Feeds, and Data Streams, installable via the Vercel skills CLI.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Converts repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant skills. It prevents instruction rot by tracing instructions to specific file lines and pinned commits.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official FLUX image generation skills providing standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2. It includes API integration patterns for webhooks, polling, and precise hex color specifications.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A registry of reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It features an OpenAPI-driven SDK and CLI for versioned installation with recursive dependency resolution.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: An execution skill for complex, multi-step tasks that features autonomous tracking and checkpoints. It includes built-in rollback procedures to ensure safe recovery during long-running tasks.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize token consumption. It utilizes a socket-based daemon architecture to support hot-reloading of server configurations.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: Enables Hermes Agent to manage Nextcloud files, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV. It ensures secure communication over HTTPS using Nextcloud App Passwords.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: A C# / .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with MCP integration. It integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based loading and supports automatic directory registration.
entries.Sahil-SS9/hermes-multichannel-prompt-opt: A plugin that rewrites and scores prompts across various surfaces like Discord and Telegram. It tailors rewrites based on model reasoning capabilities and tracks token savings in a local SQLite database.
entries.Sahil-SS9/hermaguard: An adversarial code review tool that uses three parallel subagents to attack code from different angles. A consolidator agent then triages the findings into risk-tiered reports without modifying the code.
entries.Sahil-SS9/hermes-simplify-swarm: A multi-agent swarm targeting code hygiene, clarity, and correctness to simplify codebases. It uses a risk-tiered triage system and detects language-specific anti-patterns like N+1 queries.
entries.willingning-coder/eagle-eye: An intelligent retrieval system using five layers—including FTS5 and dense embeddings—to filter 100+ skills down to the five most relevant. This reduces token consumption via a non-invasive integration.
entries.mag3nt-com/openclaw-skill: Automates payments for HTTP 402 responses using USDC across multiple protocols like Pay Link and x402. It is compatible with the agentskills.io standard, Hermes Agent, and OpenClaw.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides Nix packages for AI coding agents with daily automated updates for both source-based and binary distributions. It allows users to manage Hermes alongside other major agents like Claude Code and Goose within a reproducible Nix environment.
entries.marshallrichards/ClawPhone: ClawPhone offers automated installers and configuration guides for running agent CLIs on ARM64 Android devices via Termux. It specifically enables hardware and UI access through Termux:API and Termux:GUI, allowing Hermes to interact directly with smartphone features.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: This zero-install Windows desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 built-in tools without requiring Docker or complex setups. It features a dynamic tool maker and workflow engine, supporting local model execution via LM Studio and portable AI generation servers.
entries.AbuZar-Ansarii/Hermes-Agent-On-Android: This repository features a one-line installation script designed to deploy Hermes Agent within Android Termux environments. It prioritizes privacy and cost reduction by supporting local execution and compatibility with over 200 different models.
entries.ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart: This community Helm chart enables cloud-native Hermes deployment on Kubernetes with a focus on state-safety guardrails. It enforces single-writer persistent storage and offers configurable gateway modes with API and webhook listeners for production workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw provides a highly secure sandbox for Hermes Agent using hardware-level enforcement for network, filesystem, and process limits. It includes dynamic security policy presets and a pre-built library of over 40 integrated tools and skills.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes both the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific versions of Hermes using build arguments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services. It focuses on security through sops-nix integration for secrets handling and supports a hybrid model of Nix-managed and interactive CLI skills.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Designed for production-ready headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian, this tool configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is optimized for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integrates with the Nous Portal.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple Hermes Agent instances with distinct workspaces and identities. It facilitates enterprise-grade operations through hierarchy-aware inter-agent delegation and MCP access for external AI clients.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option provides a Docker Compose stack integrated with the Portainer UI and a ttyd web terminal for remote access. It simplifies management by deploying as a Portainer Git Repository stack with automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration layer that manages multi-agent workflows across CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen. It features a zero-dependency SQLite stack and an Aegis review system to enforce quality gates on dispatched tasks.
entries.agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires: A TUI and PWA dashboard designed to monitor and manage various coding agents, including Hermes Agent and Claude Code. It ensures execution isolation using Docker or Podman and enables parallel work via git worktrees.
entries.CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room: A governance-focused template that provides a sidecar control plane for managing everything from single VPS agents to specialist teams. It utilizes task bus patterns to structure delegation between orchestrators and specialists.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: A framework for building autonomous agent swarms with support for over 23 LLM providers, including Ollama and OpenRouter. It includes visual org charts for team orchestration alongside durable memory and scheduled execution.
entries.Agent-3-7/minions: A supervision interface for Hermes agents that uses a Kanban board to track task status and review queues. It provides live streaming of agent reasoning and requires human-in-the-loop verification for all task completions.
entries.shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room: A scaling template that uses a level-based architecture to transition from single agents to automated teams. It bundles specific skills for security auditing and VPS provisioning via a task bus delegation pattern.
entries.linke-ai/hermes-agent-team: A local collaboration system that orchestrates Leader and Specialist agents using MCP and ACP protocols. It features a real-time Web UI to monitor agent dialogues and Kanban task flows.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A specialized collective of 17 agents dedicated to research, planning, and infrastructure. The framework enforces mandatory quality gates through dedicated reviewer and tester agents with priority-based conflict resolution.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A meta-optimization harness that uses automated outer-loop search to refine agent code. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations to track performance frontiers via candidate mutation.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An agent-vs-agent arena where autonomous AI companies compete for GitHub stars. It orchestrates nine agents using Paperclip and Hermes Agent, visualizing the competition through a real-time scoreboard and task dashboard.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: An ACP-style delegation system that routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code. It prevents parent context pollution through isolated workflows and enforces safety via character limits and timeouts.

entries.colbymchenry/codegraph: A local pre-indexed code knowledge graph that minimizes token usage and tool calls by an average of 58%. It integrates via MCP server to provide multiple agents with efficient, Node.js-free codebase indexing.
entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A high-performance Rust-based TUI for monitoring token consumption across various AI coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It provides a dedicated dashboard to track and visualize costs and usage in real-time.
entries.liaohch3/claude-tap: A local trace viewer designed to inspect API traffic, prompts, and tool calls from agents like Hermes and Cursor. It features structured diffs to compare adjacent requests and reconstructs streaming responses for easier debugging.
entries.Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes: An opinionated workflow layer that acts as an autonomous CTO loop for issue triage, implementation, and QA. It includes 23 curated skills and deep integrations with Vercel, Supabase, and GitHub to streamline app deployment.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: A collection of community-driven CLI themes that customize the Hermes terminal UI with ASCII art, custom colors, and spinner verbs. Users can apply diverse aesthetics, from cyberpunk to retro DOS, using a flexible inheritance system.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: A port of the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel. This integration eliminates the need for OAuth or external processes while adding MLOps tools for vLLM and RL training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser that allows autonomous agent control through MCP. It emphasizes human-in-the-loop supervision with runtime approvals, action undo capabilities, and agent-specific bookmarks.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A TAW Agent framework that enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents with per-folder configurations. It allows users to switch seamlessly between default and project-local memories, tools, and instructions.
entries.adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman: A preconfigured setup utilizing RTK and Caveman templating to achieve 90-99% token savings. It includes over 40 specialized skills for finance and DevOps with automated installation for macOS and Linux.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: A rapid deployment stack that uses Docker to run Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It provides a zero-cost environment featuring 29 community plugins for memory and quality validation.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A deterministic static linter that scans agent configs and system prompts for failure modes without making LLM calls. It utilizes seven structural detectors and HERM v1.1 scoring for integration into CI/CD pipelines.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: A migration utility that transfers personas, memories, and skills from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It maps complex model configurations to Hermes YAML format and handles the transfer of local and system-level credentials.

entries.farion1231/cc-switch: A cross-platform manager built with Tauri 2 that provides a unified interface for Hermes Agent and other AI coding tools. It simplifies the management of multiple CLI agents across Windows, macOS, and Linux.
entries.iOfficeAI/AionUi: A local open-source cowork app that provides a GUI for Hermes Agent and over 13 other CLI agents. It features a built-in engine for web search and file access, with the ability to automatically generate editable Word, Excel, and Morph PPT files.
entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A lightweight web-based interface using Python and vanilla JavaScript that maintains full feature parity with the Hermes CLI. It utilizes a three-panel layout featuring a workspace file browser and inline previews for mobile and web access.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app that streamlines the installation and dependency resolution for local or remote Hermes Agent backends. It includes an integrated memory system supporting Mem0 and Honcho, and connects to 16 messaging gateways like Slack and WhatsApp.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A web dashboard focused on session management and channel configuration for platforms including Discord, Telegram, and Matrix. It offers local profile management with scoped memory and skills, alongside an integrated terminal and file browser.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace featuring a Monaco-powered file browser and a PTY terminal. It introduces Swarm Mode for role-based dispatch via tmux-backed workers, complemented by an MCP catalog and skills manager.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that establishes direct SSH connections to Hermes without requiring browser wrappers or daemons. It provides dedicated views for Kanban, cron jobs, and session data for a professional developer experience.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted web dashboard featuring the ZOO Swarm Monitor with a kanban pipeline and live event feed. It provides a comprehensive control plane for MCP servers, including a config editor and real-time log tailing.
entries.jazzyalex/agent-sessions: A local-first macOS session browser and cockpit designed for inspecting transcripts and tracking limits for Hermes and other coding agents. It allows users to resume CLI sessions directly in iTerm2, Warp, or the standard Terminal.
entries.nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1: A native macOS interface specifically designed for Hermes Agent instances running on SSH hosts or Orgo cloud computers. It features one-click automated installation on cloud VMs and integrated WebRTC voice mode via OpenAI Realtime.
entries.Eynzof/hermes-agent-cn-desktop: A Windows-first desktop application built with Rust and Tauri v2, tailored for the Hermes-CN-Core runtime. It includes health checks, update management, and integration with Feishu and various model providers.
entries.liftaris/herm: An alternative TUI and dashboard built with OpenTUI that renders markdown, LaTeX, and tool calls. It uses Kanban boards to monitor agentic work and provides a terminal-based interface for managing profiles and environment configs.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React SPA designed as a Telegram Mini App for local execution. It supports terminal chat with streaming responses, background agent spawning, and system health monitoring via gauges.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop GUI that requires no admin rights or Docker. It bundles over 100 tools, including ComfyUI and MusicGen, and includes a dynamic tool maker for creating Python handlers and API wrappers.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A self-hosted, glassmorphic web interface delivered as a single-file React 18 HTML application. It integrates a terminal, file browser, and real-time log streaming for managing AI skills and MCP servers.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A process monitoring dashboard that provides a real-time activity feed with cost tracking and model distribution. It allows for read-only inspection of config files and full-text search across conversation histories.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A self-hosted AI workspace featuring a chat-first interface with tool approval cards and SSE streaming. It includes a marketplace for discovering MCP servers and skills, with isolated profile management for separate API keys and memory.
entries.Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop: A multi-agent desktop client that orchestrates collaboration between 20 specialized AI agents via a PM orchestrator. It features a visual Skill Store with over 50 one-click installations and supports streaming SSE responses.
